deferred.reason: Representment-domain policies (evidence packages may contain only genuine verifiable records — never fabricated or inferred evidence; no refund on an order with an open chargeback without the duplicate check; no representment filing past the acquirer deadline; block-list additions require a confirmed pattern, not a single suspicion) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route filings, refunds, and escalations exclusively to human analysts with the Dispute Program Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Representment Package Builder, CE 3.0 Qualifier, Fight/Accept Economics Router, Pre-Arbitration Advisor, Friendly-Fraud Pattern Miner) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (PSP/acquirer dispute APIs and webhook feeds; network dispute rails via the acquirer — Visa Resolve Online, Mastercom; pre-dispute alert rails — Verifi RDR/Order Insight, Ethoca-style alert feeds; order-management/OMS and e-commerce platforms; carrier tracking APIs for delivery-confirmation evidence; device-fingerprint/antifraud platforms for CE 3.0 elements) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (submit representment/second presentment via the PSP/acquirer API, accept liability, issue the refund on the commerce platform, respond to pre-dispute alerts, accept or continue pre-arbitration, add a customer to the block-list) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous filing, refund, or block-list mutation, ever.

Golden eval sets (fight/accept triage, reason-code evidence matching, CE 3.0 qualification detection, duplicate/alert-refund detection, pre-arb economics ranking) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Merchant, Customer, Order, Payment, Chargeback, ReasonCode, Representment, PreArbitration, EvidencePackage, PreDisputeAlert, BlockListEntry, MonitoringProgramStatus) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the representment_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (chargeback_response, evidence_assembly, pre_arbitration_review, friendly_fraud_review, alert_deduplication) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (representment_win_probability, friendly_fraud_score) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest order anomaly + xgboost win-likelihood scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Chargeback", "Representment", "Response due", "CE 3.0 qualified", "Below fight threshold", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
